# openshift_pxe_server (Resource)

Deploys a PXE server on a bastion host via SSH. Installs dnsmasq (DHCP proxy + TFTP) and nginx (HTTP), extracts the kernel and initrd from the discovery ISO, and serves them to nodes on the provisioning network. Nodes boot, register with the Assisted Installer, and install automatically. No BMC license required. Requires unbonded NICs during boot.



## Example Usage

### With Assisted Installer — discovery ISO served over PXE

The discovery ISO URL comes from `openshift_cluster.discovery_iso_url`. The PXE server extracts the kernel and initrd from the ISO and serves them via dnsmasq + nginx. Nodes boot, register with the Assisted Installer, and installation proceeds automatically.

**Note:** PXE boot requires that each node's NIC is **not** configured as an LACP bond at boot time. The LACP bond is not up during the UEFI PXE handshake, so the DHCP offer is never received. If your nodes use bonded NICs, use `openshift_bmc_boot` with an Agent ISO instead.

```hcl
resource "openshift_cluster" "prod" {
  cluster_name         = "prod"
  base_dns_domain      = "example.internal"
  openshift_version    = "4.16"
  pull_secret          = var.pull_secret
  ssh_public_key       = var.ssh_public_key

  api_vip              = "192.168.10.10"
  ingress_vip          = "192.168.10.11"
  machine_network_cidr = "192.168.10.0/24"

  create_infra_env = true
  auto_install     = false
}

resource "openshift_pxe_server" "bastion" {
  bastion_host    = "bastion.example.internal"
  bastion_user    = "core"
  bastion_ssh_key = file("~/.ssh/bastion_id_rsa")

  # Discovery ISO from the Assisted Installer.
  iso_url = openshift_cluster.prod.discovery_iso_url

  interface       = "ens3"          # provisioning network NIC on the bastion
  dhcp_range_start = "192.168.10.100"
  dhcp_range_end   = "192.168.10.200"
}
```

### With Agent-Based Installer ISO

Use `iso_path` (the path on the bastion where the ISO was uploaded) instead of `iso_url`. Set `bastion_host` on `openshift_agent_iso` to upload the ISO automatically.

**Note:** Same limitation applies — PXE does not work if nodes have LACP-bonded NICs. Use `openshift_bmc_boot` in that case.

```hcl
resource "openshift_agent_iso" "prod" {
  install_config = file("install-config.yaml")
  agent_config   = file("agent-config.yaml")

  bastion_host    = "bastion.example.internal"
  bastion_user    = "core"
  bastion_ssh_key = file("~/.ssh/bastion_id_rsa")
  bastion_iso_dir = "/var/www/html/isos"
}

resource "openshift_pxe_server" "bastion" {
  bastion_host    = "bastion.example.internal"
  bastion_user    = "core"
  bastion_ssh_key = file("~/.ssh/bastion_id_rsa")

  # Path on the bastion where the ISO was uploaded.
  iso_path = openshift_agent_iso.prod.bastion_iso_url

  interface        = "ens3"
  dhcp_range_start = "192.168.10.100"
  dhcp_range_end   = "192.168.10.200"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `bastion_host` (String) Hostname or IP of the bastion host.
- `bastion_ssh_key` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded SSH private key for the bastion host.

### Optional

- `bastion_port` (Number) SSH port on the bastion host.
- `bastion_user` (String) SSH user on the bastion host.
- `dhcp_range_end` (String) End of DHCP range for PXE clients, e.g. 10.0.1.200.
- `dhcp_range_start` (String) Start of DHCP range for PXE clients, e.g. 10.0.1.100. Leave empty to use proxy DHCP (no range allocation).
- `http_port` (Number) Port for the nginx HTTP server serving the ISO/kernel/initrd.
- `interface` (String) Network interface on the bastion to serve PXE on (provisioning network).
- `iso_path` (String) Path to a locally-built ISO on the bastion (use openshift_agent_iso.iso_path for Agent-Based).
- `iso_url` (String) URL of the discovery ISO (use openshift_cluster.discovery_iso_url for Assisted Installer).

### Read-Only

- `pxe_server_url` (String) Base URL of the PXE HTTP server, e.g. http://bastion.example.internal:8080.
